metadata.title: Hazard Categories
metadata.description: This dataset contains information about various hazard categories associated with explosive events.

hazardDivisions.name: Mass Explosion Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Substances and articles which have a mass explosion hazard.

hazardDivisions.name: Fragment Producing Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Explosive substances and articles which have a projection hazard producing dangerous fragments.

hazardDivisions.name: Fragment Producing Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Explosive substances and articles which have a projection hazard without significant blast effects.

hazardDivisions.name: Fragment Producing Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Explosive substances and articles which have a projection hazard with limited fragment range.

hazardDivisions.name: Propellant Explosive
hazardDivisions.description: More hazardous HD 1.3 propellant explosives which present a significant mass fire hazard and intense thermal radiation.

source.paragraph: Derived from Part 1, Chapter 1, Table 1 and QD framework.

hazardDivisions.name: Non-Propellant HD 1.3 Items
hazardDivisions.description: Less hazardous HD 1.3 items and substances which present a fire hazard but are not classified as propellant explosives.

source.paragraph: Derived from Part 1, Chapter 1, Table 1 and QD framework.

hazardDivisions.name: Minor Explosion Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Substances and articles which present no significant hazard beyond the package.

hazardDivisions.name: Very Insensitive Mass Explosion Hazard
hazardDivisions.description: Very insensitive substances having a mass explosion hazard.

hazardDivisions.name: Extremely Insensitive Articles
hazardDivisions.description: Extremely insensitive articles which do not have a mass explosion hazard.
